cohort_size: Qwen3: 35 rows (5 ZS + 30 trained). Qwen2.5: 6 ZS rows kept for comparison. Llama: 25 rows so far (5 ZS + 20 trained), 5 cells remaining.
verdict_summary: Qwen3-VL fully completed; rule passes with rho=-1.000 across 5 cats (lift range +0.197 to +0.443). Llama-3.2 partial; on the same 5 cats, lifts are uniformly tiny (-0.004 to +0.021). Verdict statuses on multi-model rows TBD until /verdict run on the cumulative cohort.
diagnosis: Three model families tested on the same 5-category subset (capsule, tile, wood, zipper, chewinggum) under identical recipe (rank=8, lr=2e-4, epochs=3): Gemma 4 E4B-it, Qwen3-VL-8B, Llama-3.2-11B-Vision. Headline observation: the rule (lift inversely correlates with ZS-AUROC) passes with maximum strength on Gemma and Qwen3 (rho=-1.000 on each, on the same 5 cats), but appears underpowered on Llama where all 4 cells so far show lifts in [-0.004, +0.021]. Critical detail: Llama's ZS profile on the test subset is unusually high (4 of 5 cats with ZS>=0.63, 2 of those near-ceiling at 0.979 and 0.997), which leaves no headroom for FT lift. The qualitative direction of the rule (high ZS -> tiny lift) IS satisfied on Llama, but the rank-correlation test cannot distinguish the rule's prediction from baseline noise when lift variance collapses to ~0.025.

model_specific_observations.Gemma_4_E4B-it: Already-characterized on 24 cats (Pass 4): rho=-0.778, p<10^-5. Mean cell time ~5-12 min. Stable across 3 seeds. Recipe (rank=8, lr=2e-4) appears well-suited.
model_specific_observations.Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct: rho=-1.000 on 5 cats. Per-cat reproducibility is striking: capsule N=2 trio in [0.967, 0.968], tile N=2 trio in [0.993, 0.994], wood trio at 1.000 across all seeds, zipper trio in [0.909, 0.911], chewinggum trio in [0.978, 0.984]. Cell time ~5 min. The TRL/transformers token-obfuscation bug needed a workaround patch (commit 376a4fb) — without it, Qwen FT cannot start.
model_specific_observations.Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct: All 4 trained cells so far show lifts <=0.025. capsule trio at [0.643, 0.654, 0.649] vs ZS=0.630, lift +0.020 mean. Tile trio at [0.970, 0.974, 0.979] vs ZS=0.979, lift -0.001 mean (slight regression). Wood trio at [0.997, 0.998, 0.999] vs ZS=0.997, at-ceiling. Zipper trio at [0.759, 0.769, 0.775] vs ZS=0.768, lift +0.001. Cell time ~7-8 min (slower than Qwen3 due to 11B params). Two interpretations of the uniformly-tiny-lift pattern: (a) Llama's high-ZS profile on this subset means there's nothing for FT to add per the rule, (b) Llama-3.2's LoRA/recipe interaction differs from Gemma+Qwen and may need different hyperparameters.

recommendation: next_config: Path 2 — characterize Llama's ZS across all 24 Tier A cats (~19 ZS-only cells, ~1h compute), then pick 5 lowest-ZS cats for Llama and run trained sweep at N=2 with 3 seeds (~15 cells, ~1.5h compute). Total ~2.5-3h. This converts the current ambiguous Llama signal (rule's qualitative prediction holds, quantitative test is underpowered) into a real test of the rule on Llama-specific lift-headroom cats. Either outcome is valuable: rule confirms on Llama -> three-family confirmation, or rule fails on Llama-low-ZS -> documented model-architecture boundary.
rationale: The Gemma+Qwen3 evidence (both rho=-1.000 on same 5 cats) is unambiguous: the rule transfers across these two model families in both qualitative direction and quantitative form. The Llama signal is more nuanced — uniformly-tiny lifts are simultaneously consistent with the rule's qualitative direction (high ZS -> tiny lift, all 4 cells obey this) AND uninformative for the rank-correlation test. Resolving this ambiguity requires either accepting the partial evidence (workshop-acceptable) OR running the targeted Llama-low-ZS test (path 2). Path 2 has higher ceiling (3-family confirmation if rule holds) and approximately the same expected value as path 3 (drop Llama). Recommended given the user's stated preference for the strongest possible result.
anti_goodhart_check: All trained cells per (model, cat, N) trio show seed-noise of <=0.020 AUROC, consistent with seed-stable training. No single-seed cell drives any conclusion. The Llama partial result is being reported AS-IS; not retroactively softening the rule's quantitative form to absorb the Llama data into a passing claim.
next_action_sequence: Wait for current Llama 5-cat sweep to finish (~30 min, 5 cells remaining as of timestamp).
Launch Llama ZS on 19 remaining Tier A cats (recipe: v0.5-tier-a-llama32-zs).
Analyze Llama 24-cat ZS profile; pick 5 lowest-ZS cats for the rule re-test.
Launch Llama trained sweep on those 5 cats × N=2 × 3 seeds (recipe: v0.5-tier-a-llama32-low-zs).
Compute per-model Spearman rho across each model's appropriate test subset.
Run /verdict on the cumulative multi-model cohort.
Update queue.json to mark multi-model phase completed; commit data + this review's siblings (final close-out).
